column1: # Scale / precipitation remediation knowledge base (screening level).
# Maps a deposit type to candidate dissolver/solvent treatments for cleaning fouled or plugged equipment
# (valves, trim, bridles, lines, exchangers). Used by neqsim.process.chemistry.scale.ScaleRemediationAdvisor
# for root-cause-analysis proposed-solution generation. NOT a substitute for a vendor/field treatment program.
# Pipe-delimited so free text may contain commas. Effectiveness: HIGH

column3: Hydrochloric acid (HCl) with corrosion inhibitor and iron-control agent
column5: Acid soak or circulation, then neutralise and flush
column9: Corrosive; CO2 evolution can pressurise; mandatory corrosion inhibitor and spent-acid neutralisation; verify metallurgy
Slower than acid but far less corrosive; check chelant biodegradability and disposal

column3: HCl with iron-control (reducing) agent or DTPA chelant
column9: Iron control prevents Fe(OH)3/Fe2O3 re-precipitation of spent fluid

column3: HCl with H2S control and iron stabiliser
column9: Releases toxic H2S - H2S control and gas detection mandatory; pyrophoric residue risk
Lower H2S release than acid; biocide handling and disposal controls

column3: High-pH chelant (DTPA/EDTA at pH~12) with synergist
column5: Hot soak, long contact, multiple stages
column9: Acid-insoluble; slow dissolution; milling/mechanical removal often required
Acid-insoluble; slow; often co-precipitated with BaSO4

column3: Carbonate/hydroxide converter followed by HCl, or EDTA chelant
column9: Anhydrite dissolves slowly; conversion boosts acid solubility
Simplest remedy; manage brine disposal and avoid re-scaling on cooling

column3: Proprietary dithiazine dissolver (glycolic-acid or oxidiser based) with surfactant
column9: Amorphous triazine-scavenger reaction product; RESTORE water pH control (7.5-9.5) and review scavenger dose to prevent recurrence
Only partial; mechanical trim/bridle cleaning usually still needed
Flammable aromatic - ventilation and hot-work controls; dispersant limits re-deposition

column5: Hot-oil circulation above wax appearance temperature
column9: Thermal + solvent; monitor for re-deposition on cooling downstream
Calcium-naphthenate soap; upstream low-pH/demulsifier control reduces formation

column3: Sample and identify deposit (XRD/SEM-EDX) before selecting solvent
column9: Do not select a dissolver blindly - acid dissolves carbonate/sulfide but not sulfate scale; identify first
